segment-text: The Sandwich Days
Written by
And
Phil Vaira & Andrea Walters BLACK.
INT. ELEMENTARY CLASSROOM - FLASHBACK - MORNING

Several fifth graders talk among each other before the beginning of class. The sound is muffled. YOUNG BILLIAM (11) does not talk and looks lonely at his desk. He draws on some paper being quite pleased with his work. Some children accidently push a desk against his causing his pencil to move upwards. His picture is ruined with a long straight line in the middle. His little, personal world is disrupted with the bang causing the muffled talking around him turn instantly clear. MRS. ZIMMERMAN stands at the front of the class taking attendance.
Okay, everybody settle down now. It may be the first day in class, and I know we"re all excited here. Let"s see if I can learn your names as I take role.
The fifth graders quiet down while the standing children sit down at their desks. MRS. ZIMMERMAN
JENNY raises her hand.
Here!
MRS. ZIMMERMAN
Here.
MRS. ZIMMERMAN
I"m here. MRS. ZIMMERMAN Heather Levington?
Here.
MRS. ZIMMERMAN
Here!
YOUNG BILLIAM is growing nervous while he begins sinking down his chair. MRS. ZIMMERMAN Ashley Nelson?
Here!
MRS. ZIMMERMAN
Here!
MRS. ZIMMERMAN

No one knows who YOUNG BILLIAM is but do notice someone sinking down in his chair. Some laugh at the name. YOUNG TERRENCE finds it amusing while lightly jokes to his peers. MRS. ZIMMERMAN looks around. MRS. ZIMMERMAN (CONT'D)
YOUNG BILLIAM continues to sink.
INT. REP"S OFFICE - PRESENT - AFTERNOON
BILLIAM (18) sits across from a well dressed, middle aged admissions representative. The rep has a note pad in his lap and a pen in his hand, looking attentively at BILLIAM.
Yeah.
Could be worse. I once knew a kid who"s parents named him Bobert.
Really?
Yeah. (laughs) We mocked the hell outta that kid! (continues to laugh) BILLIAM is unamused, and the admissions rep notices this.
(clears his throat) Sorry. Please, continue.
Why do you want to know so much about my childhood? Well, when we ask most people to talk about their childhood on this form they at least give us something. You just wrote "Not Applicable".
BILLIAM nods slightly.
(Agreeing) Mmmhmm. Would you really describe your childhood as "Not applicable"?
BILLIAM opens his mouth as if he"s about to say something, thinking very hard.
INT. LUNCH ROOM - FLASHBACK
YOUNG BILLIAM sits at a lunchroom table, all alone.
Back then, kids were (pause) - less than civil towards me.

YOUNG BILLIAM suddenly gets hits directly in the face with a large, sloppy sandwich. Shocked, he wipes the contents of the sandwich from his face as loud laughter erupts from off screen. Seated across the lunchroom is YOUNG TERRENCE, the source of the laughter. YOUNG TERRENCE sits amongst his lackeys.
Terrence was my constant tormentor.
YOUNG TERRENCE retrieves another sandwich from his paper lunchsack.
YOUNG TERRENCE begins to happily eat his next sandwich.
INT. CLASSROOM - FLASHBACK - CONTINUOUS
YOUNG BILLIAM sits in class and gets hit with a sandwich in the back of the head. YOUNG TERRENCE"S laughter erupts behind him.
INT. BATHROOM - FLASHBACK - CONTINUOUS
YOUNG BILLIAM stands at the urinal and gets hit with another sandwich. YOUNG TERRENCE LAUGHS off-screen.
INT. GYM - FLASHBACK - CONTINUOUS
YOUNG BILLIAM prepares to catch a kicked ball in the back of the gym and gets hit with a ball from behind by the laughing unseen YOUNG TERRENCE.
Well, c'mon, he couldn't always throw sandwiches. That's just ridiculous.
INT. HOUSE - FLASHBACK - CONTINUOUS
But my mother was always there for me when I got home. And she always tried to make me feel better about the situation.
Oh, Billiam.
YOUNG BILLIAM"S mother picks a piece of tomato out of his hair.
Do you know why the kids do this to you?
(sniffling) Because my name's Billiam?
Oh, heavens no. It's because they're jealous of you.
YOUNG BILLIAM lifts his head, displaying a look of hope.
Really?
His mother nods, smiling. YOUNG BILLIAM smiles back.
INT. REP"S OFFICE - PRESENT
What were they jealous of? The friends I didn"t have? The social skills I was unable to acquire?
Well, your mom sounds like a pretty smart person. I mean, my mom told me the same thing, and I... (look of realization) Oh..
The worst part was that I actually listened to her. (thinks) Wait a minute, no. That"s not the worst part. The worst part was...
INT. CLASSROOM - FLASHBACK

YOUNG BILLIAM walks to his seat, and YOUNG TERRENCE sticks his foot out to trip him. YOUNG BILLIAM falls with a thud. YOUNG TIM, who sits all alone in the corner, watches this. YOUNG BILLIAM comes back to his feet and turns to YOUNG
You know what, Terrence? I know why you pick on me.
Oh, really?
You"re just jealous of me, and you pick on me because you"re really insecure
about yourself, and need to make me feel bad to make yourself feel better.
YOUNG TIM, hearing this, shuts his eyes wincing. YOUNG TERRENCE pauses as he takes this all in.
(sarcastic) You know what? You're right. I am jealous of you. All I really want to be is your friend.
Really?
YOUNG TERRENCE reaches into his pocket and pulls out a sandwich, throwing it in YOUNG BILLIAM"S face. He walks away, and YOUNG BILLIAM stands there, shocked.
My first run-in with sarcasm.
YOUNG TIM shakes his head, getting up from his seat and walking over to YOUNG BILLIAM. He pulls out some tissues from his pocket and hands them to YOUNG BILLIAM.
And that"s when I first met my best friend, Tim.
He"s not really jealous of you, you know.
But my mom said he was.
Well, there"s your first problem. When it comes to bullies, never listen to your mother.
YOUNG BILLIAM thinks about this.
He was right. And the more I thought about it, the more I remembered all the horrible advice my mother had given me.
INT. BILLIAM"S LIVING ROOM - FLASHBACK
YOUNG BILLIAM is looking up to his mother. She is cutting onions in the kitchen.
Just ignore them, and they'll stop picking on you.
YOUNG BILLIAM is counseled by his mother at the dining room table.
The next time they pick on you, you just tell your teacher. She'll make everything better.
YOUNG BILLIAM is leaning against the wall in his bathrobe as his mother talks in the background.
I think it's time I gave their parents a call. Once they get a good talking to, they'll stop picking on you.
MOM is tucking in YOUNG BILLIAM in his bed. It is night.
He won"t bother you anymore, much like that boogeyman you mentioned before hiding under your bed. Goodnight dear.

MOM gets up and walks towards the door. As she does, YOUNG BILLIAM"s eyes are wide open with a sense of realization of an old fear. While finding enough courage, he grabs his flashlight, turns it on, and slowly looks under the bed. The flashlight shines towards a body under the bed appearing to be YOUNG TERRENCE. He holds a sandwich in his right hand and moves it towards him. YOUNG BILLIAM wakes up in a sweat screaming.
Mom! The boogeyman is Terrence! INT. REP"S OFFICE - PRESENT

Her heart was in the right place, but she just didn't understand what it was really like. Sure bullies were probably insecure with themselves which caused them to pick on those lesser than them, but knowing that didn't help as a little kid. In the end, there are just no good ways to deal with a bully.
BILLIAM smiles deviously.
But there are some bad ones.
INT. SCHOOL CAFETERIA - FLASHBACK
YOUNG BILLIAM and YOUNG TIM sit at a lunchroom table, eating.
Tim and I basically became inseparable for the next several months. I"d like to say by that time I"d taken care of my Terrence situation, but I can say that things were at least starting to change.
YOUNG BILLIAM gets hit with a sandwich, which smears across his face and falls on the table. He remains unfazed.
Hmm. Terrence"s mother switched to Miracle Whip.
He wipes off his face with a nearby napkin, and continues eating.
At least she stopped using toothpicks. (pause) You ever going to stand up to him?
Well, my mom says I should try not to
give him a big reaction.
Your mom?! You"re still listening to your mom?! Are you kidding? Nothing she"s said has worked, Bill! Seriously, you need to do something. If you don't, he"ll never stop. EVER!
I know.
YOUNG BILLIAM sits there and thinks to himself.
Someone needs to hit him with a sandwich.
YOUNG BILLIAM suddenly gets a look of realization across his face.
I"ve got it.
What?
We're going to settle this on the playground.
YOUNG TIM looks confused.
EXT. BILLIAM"S HOUSE - FLASHBACK - AFTERNOON

YOUNG BILLIAM and YOUNG TIM walk out from the back door in a hurry wearing backpacks filled with sandwiches. Attached to their pants are walky-talkies. They begin to head towards the garage until a car door shuts. YOUNG BILLIAM moves his arm to stop YOUNG TIM from proceeding further. They hide around the corner from being seen. BILLIAM peeks and sees his mother walking towards the front door with a bag of groceries.
Civilian hostile spotted.
Should we initiate plan B?
YOUNG BILLIAM turns towards YOUNG TIM.
Affirmative. Get ready.
The boys detach their walky-talkies from their pants and turn them on.
Tim, your objective is to watch her every move. You can reach me on here.
Will do.
Okay. Go!

The boys set ways. YOUNG BILLIAM"S mother walks into the house. YOUNG BILLIAM makes his way towards the garage while YOUNG TIM starts sneaking by the house windows. YOUNG BILLIAM brings the walky-talkie towards his mouth. His head peeks around the corner for a clear visual. YOUNG BILLIAM Tango Lee Seven this is Bravo Whiskey Six. Radio check, over!
Bravo Whiskey Six, this is Tango Lima Seven. I read you loud and clear, over!
Roger, Tango Lee Seven. Give me a sitrep, over!
YOUNG TIM looks into the window: she is dancing with a cat. YOUNG TIM Target is in sight. YOUNG TIM rolls his eyes and gets back into a hiding position.
Be advised, crazy mom close, over.
YOUNG BILLIAM makes it to the garage and quietly sneaks towards the garage door. He speaks into the walky-talkie again. YOUNG BILLIAM
Tango Lima Seven, I"m in position, over.

YOUNG TIM peeks through the window again but does not see her. YOUNG TIM All clear, Bravo Whiskey Six. Mission is a go. I repeat, mission is a go! YOUNG BILLIAM Roger, Wilco! Go mission! Over and out! YOUNG BILLIAM grabs the garage door handle and opens it. He runs in as YOUNG TIM follows him from behind. They run out with a ladder, tape, and a long rope. YOUNG BILLIAM"s mother barely recognizes them just in time as they slowly run off with a large ladder.
EXT. PLAYGROUND - FLASHBACK - AFTERNOON
YOUNG BILLIAM is on a ladder as TIM hands him the next backpack of sandwiches. A rope is attached to a closed net attached to the hoop. Duck tape is below the hoop in a formation of an ,,X".
You sure this is a good idea?
No, I think it"s a bad one! You want out?
No. I was just thinking of one sandwich. I think we made a hundred sandwiches!

Revenge is messy. You said it yourself, Tim. I have to stand up to Terrence. If I don"t, he"ll never stop. I tried telling the teacher. I tried ignoring him. I tried to look brave. I tried to pretend I don"t care. But I do
care. It"s time for Terrence to get a taste of his own medicine.
What if it doesn"t work?
It will work.
EXT. PLAYGROUND - FLASHBACK - AFTERNOON

YOUNG BILLIAM and YOUNG TIM stand at the playground to their old school, waiting. YOUNG TIM glances at his watch. YOUNG TIM Maybe he won"t show. YOUNG BILLIAM He"ll show. Suddenly in the distance, YOUNG TERRENCE shows up on his bike. The scene plays in slow motion. He ride up to the playground and tosses his bike to the ground, his long coat tails flutter in the wind (in slow motion a la John Woo). YOUNG BILLIAM and YOUNG TIM watch this. YOUNG TERRENCE approaches the duo.
Well, well, well... if it isn"t Billiam Williams.
YOUNG BILLIAM and YOUNG TIM exchange glances.
And you brought your friend Dim Tim.
Hi Terry.
I got your note. What do you want?
I just want to teach you a lesson.
Oh yeah? And what"s that?
That when someone you"ve tortured for his entire life asks you to meet him out in a secluded area(pause) you should always watch where you stand.

YOUNG TERRENCE looks down to find that he is standing on a giant X. YOUNG BILLIAM pulls a string. YOUNG TERRENCE sees YOUNG BILLIAM pull the string and looks up as a giant bucket of sandwiches is released above his head. A hundred sandwiches fall out of the bucket and fall all over YOUNG TERRENCE. YOUNG TERRENCE slowly looks up from his feet at YOUNG BILLIAM and YOUNG TIM.
You're dead.
As they move forward, YOUNG BILLIAM and YOUNG TIM start backing up.
Uh, Bill? What was your plan for after we hit him with the sandwiches?
That"s about as far as I got.
YOUNG TERRENCE backs them both into a tree, grabbing YOUNG BILLIAM and raising his fist.
(smiling) I"m really going to enjoy this.

YOUNG BILLIAM winces, waiting for impact when in the distance a car engine is heard revving. YOUNG TERRRENCE slowly turns his head around. Far in the distance, a silver station wagon sits at the end of the parking lot. The engine revs again. YOUNG TERRENCE lets go of his grip and walks towards the car.
Now, what"s this?
Bill? Is that your mother?
YOUNG BILLIAM simply stares ahead, nodding in shock. YOUNG TERRENCE squints towards the car to get a better look. He laughs to himself.
Aww, isn't that cute? Billy called his mommy to come and save him.
The car engine revs again.
INT. BILLIAM'S MOM'S CAR - DAY
Nobody hurts my boy and gets away
EXT. ELEMENTARY SCHOOL PLAYGROUND - DAY

The car suddenly peels forward, towards YOUNG TERRENCE. YOUNG TERRENCE"s face drops in horror, as he begins to run in the opposite direction. He runs towards his bike and hops on, pedaling away. YOUNG TERRENCE cowardly screams as he drives his bike fast away, YOUNG BILLIAM"s mother on his tail. They drive off into the distance and disappear, leaving YOUNG BILLIAM and YOUNG TIM alone.
EXT. ELEMENTARY SCHOOL PLAYGROUND - DAY
Wow. (pause) Your mom is insane. YOUNG BILLIAM smiles, as he stares off to where they drove off.
Naw, she just loves me.
YOUNG BILLIAM pats YOUNG TIM on the back. They walk off.
INT. REP"S OFFICE - PRESENT
And, so that"s how it ended. Terrence ended up transferring to a new school, and I really haven"t seen him since.
The ADMISSION REP places his folder on the table.
Well, I must say, that"s one heck of a story. And I can see why you didn"t try and fit that into a single essay.
The ADMISSION REP sticks out his hand.
We"ll let you know.
INT. LOBBY - PRESENT -CONTINUOUS
BILLIAM walks out into the lobby and looks around to see several students sitting in a line of chairs. At the front of them is TERRENCE. He looks up and notices BILLIAM, surprised.
Terrence?
Oh hey, Bill.
You"re going to this school?
If they let me.
Awkward pause as the two think of what to say.
Well, good luck Terrence.
Hey, you too.
BILLIAM begins to walk away.
Hey Billiam!
BILLIAM shields his face in terror, wincing. He looks back to see TERRENCE is sticking out his hand, smiling.
Good to see you.
BILLIAM shakes TERRENCE"s hand.
You too.
Hey, maybe we can get together and get a bite to eat sometime. No sandwiches of course. (laughs)
Yeah, I"d like that.
EXT. RESTRAUNT - DAY
BILLIAM and TERRENCE are talking to each other as they eat by a window. Laughs are exchanged as they recall stories.
EXT. ROAD - DAY
BILLIAM walks with TERENCE down the sidewalk.
It was great seeing you again. My car is right up there. Where is yours?
Oh, it"s at the shop. There were some problems with the engine and had to get
it fixed.
That sucks, man. Need a ride?
Uh, my mom is picking me up.
TERRENCE is in shock.
She"s... what?
BILLIAM looks at him questionably.
She"s picking me up. I told her to arrive about this time.
BILLIAM glances around.
She should be here... oh, there she is.
TERRENCE looks where BILLIAM is looking at. He spots his mother standing outside the car. Her eyes lock on TERRENCE with an evil grin.
You!

TERRENCE panics and begins running the opposite direction and around the corner. BILLIAM is confused and watches him run off. BILLIAM"S MOTHER gets in the car quickly and turns it on, storming after TERRENCE.
